phrasal_verbs.meaning: start suddenly (for a war, fire, etc)
stop doing sth you do regularly
start to suffer from a minor illness
do less of (smoking, etc); reduce an amount of
stop being effective (for a drug, etc)

A.title: Topic vocabulary in contrast
A.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

questions.sentence: After the check-up, the specialist wrote me a ...... for antibiotics.
Could you share the ...... for that delicious energy bar you brought to the gym?
Following the sports injury, the athlete spent three months in physical .......
Scientists around the world are still searching for a ...... for Alzheimer's disease.
Honey and lemon is a classic home ...... for a sore throat.
The nurse asked me to roll up my sleeve so she could ...... the bruise.
Health officials have been called in to ...... the outbreak of food poisoning at the school canteen.
She's booked in for an ...... to repair the torn ligament in her knee.
The consultant told him he would need ...... on his spine to relieve the pressure.
My throat is really ...... - I think I'm coming down with something.
Watch your step on those wet tiles or you'll ...... yourself!
I've had a sharp ...... in my lower back ever since I lifted those heavy boxes.
The young patient looked worryingly pale and **thin / slim** - the nurse was concerned about his rapid weight loss.
A **fit / healthy** diet rich in whole grains and vegetables is essential for long-term wellbeing.
After touching some plants in the garden, she developed a red **fever / rash** all over her forearms.
The physiotherapist wrapped a **bandage / plaster** tightly around the runner's sprained ankle.
Always clean a wound thoroughly to reduce the risk of **infection / pollution**.
The new painkiller began to show its **results / effects** within about twenty minutes.
The consultant strolled through the **ward / clinic**, stopping briefly to check on each patient.
Three cyclists were **injured / damaged** when a car ran a red light at the junction.
It's actually beneficial for children to catch minor **diseases / illnesses**, such as colds, as it helps build up their immunity.
The label on the cough syrup stated that the recommended adult **dose / fix** should not exceed 10 ml.

C.title: Phrasal verbs (transformation)
C.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

questions.sentence:
Elena had to cancel her marathon training because she caught the flu. **down**
Elena ...... which meant she had to cancel her marathon training.

Doctors agree that quitting cigarettes is one of the most effective things you can do for your lungs. **up**
Doctors agree that if you ...... your lungs will benefit enormously.

The vet recommended ending our elderly cat's suffering. **put**
The vet recommended ...... to end her suffering.
questions.answer: to put the dog down/to put down the dog

questions.sentence:
I haven't got the energy to go for a swim today. **up**
I don't really ...... swimming today.

The doctor explained that stress can trigger a migraine. **on**
The doctor explained that stress can ...... a migraine.

A measles epidemic started suddenly in the spring and many children fell ill. **out**
Many children fell ill when the measles epidemic ...... in the spring.

D.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
D.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: After the general anaesthetic, it took me a while to come ....... and realise where I was.
The nutritionist advised her to ...... down on sugar and processed snacks.
The local anaesthetic started to wear ...... about two hours after the dental procedure.
Without regular exercise, it's surprisingly easy to end up ...... on extra weight.
The heat and dehydration got to him during the race and he ...... out on the track.
It took her several weeks to ...... over the chest infection.
The surgeons weren't sure my uncle was going to pull ......, but he made a full recovery.
Could you ...... after my elderly neighbour while I'm in hospital?
The health board urgently needs to find a/an ...... to the overcrowding in the emergency department.
The receptionist pointed out that I had ...... my appointment and the slot had been given to someone else.
The paramedics who rushed into the collapsed building were in ...... of their lives.
With such a hectic schedule, I barely manage to ...... any exercise during the working week.
Regular swimming is an excellent way to ...... fit while protecting your joints.
The doctor suggested that a few days of complete rest by the sea would do you a lot of .......
Before travelling to the tropics, the nurse gave me an ...... against yellow fever.
The pharmacist showed me how to ...... the ointment evenly over the affected area.
After six months of rehabilitation, she was finally starting to get back into .......
Health officials were alarmed by the ...... of the virus to neighbouring countries.
He admitted he had become ...... to painkillers during his long recovery from back surgery.
The surgical team is scheduled to ...... on her damaged shoulder first thing tomorrow morning.
Researchers say that people who rarely exercise are likely **E** to develop heart problems in later life.
Have you heard that short-sightedness can now be operated **A** on with laser technology?
My sister is getting really tired of **D** reminding her teenage son to take his medication.
The physio suggested you try **B** stretching for ten minutes every morning.
Your doctor is right - it really is worth **C** cutting back on salt for the sake of your blood pressure.

G.title: Cloze (Word patterns)
G.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: Many parents today are worried ...... the lack of physical activity among teenagers.
Young people are increasingly becoming addicted ...... screens, which is having a negative impact on their sleep and mental health.
But what leads people ...... making such unhealthy choices in the first place?
What, for example, makes someone inject a substance ...... their bloodstream when they know it could cause serious harm?
Is it because they struggle to cope ...... the pressures of modern life?
Fitness professionals often complain ...... the shortage of affordable sports facilities in poorer communities.
Everyone would benefit ...... having more open green spaces where people can exercise freely.

H.title: Word formation (sentences)
H.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

questions.sentence: The young athlete was completely ...... (AWARE) that overtraining could seriously damage his joints.
My colleague is ...... (ALLERGY) to penicillin, so the doctor had to prescribe a different antibiotic.
Her ...... (ILL) turned out to be far more serious than anyone had initially thought.
Hikers were warned to avoid touching several ...... (POISON) plants found along the mountain trail.
Wearing the neck brace throughout the summer was extremely ...... (COMFORT), but the physiotherapist insisted it was essential.
The gym instructor assessed each member's ...... (FIT) levels before creating a personalised training programme.
Fortunately, the rugby player's ...... (INJURE) were not serious enough to require surgery.
The physiotherapist designed a set of exercises specifically to ...... (STRONG) the muscles around her knee.

I.title: Word formation (text)
I.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

questions.sentence: Nowadays, it appears there is an ...... (OPERATE) available to treat almost any physical complaint.
From reshaping body contours to helping patients ...... (EMPHASISE) their natural strengths, private ...... (SURGERY) are always ready to offer their services.
At VitaClinic, however, we understand that patients dread the ...... (COMFORT) that comes with going under the knife.
Surely the only way to achieve those ...... (BENEFIT) results is to have an invasive procedure, right?
Wrong! Our approach avoids the need for any ...... (SURGERY) intervention and eliminates the lengthy ...... (RECOVER) time that follows traditional operations.
Instead, we offer a tailored programme of ...... (INJECT) that gradually reshape and rejuvenate your appearance.
To find out more, simply call and ask to speak with one of our expert ...... (OPERATE).
